output.code:
# Read the file as bytes and show repr and length and portions for analysis
path = "/mnt/data/file-M5syhXhoq3x9tXVUfXAd4B-text_to_analyse.txt"
with open(path, "rb") as f:
    data = f.read()
len_data = len(data)
data[:4000], len_data, data.decode('utf-8')[:4000]

s = data.decode('utf-8')
start = s.find("إن الله")
start, s[start:start+200]

start = s.find("إن الله")
end = len(s)
span_text = s[start:end]
# Trim only edge characters if present (list provided). Remove leading/trailing unerlying 
# But spec says trim only whitespace, newlines, tabs, RTL controls, quotes «»"' and brackets ()[]{} <> and punctuation ، ؛ : . , ! ? … and tatweel ـ.
import re
# Create a regex to trim those at edges
trim_chars = r'[\s\u200E\u200F\u202A\u202B\u202C\u202D\u202E«»"\'\(\)\[\]\{\}<>،؛:\.\,!؟…\u0640]+'
# But must only trim at edges
span_trimmed = re.sub(rf'^{trim_chars}|{trim_chars}$', '', span_text)
span_trimmed[:200], len(span_trimmed), span_trimmed == span_text, span_text.endswith("الص"), span_text[:10]
